what_it_does: Records a raised signal notice that the operator decided the global visual-fidelity decision with the chosen option 'Push the drawn style further first', and states that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity can resume.
observations: It is a signal-type noticeboard item sourced from claude_code with state 'raised'
It links via attached_to to decision://global/visual-fidelity
Created and updated timestamps are identical
History contains a single transition from null to raised by operator with note 'filed'
The body explicitly calls the resume step the lane's/operator's gated step
standouts: This is a decision.decided signal, not code or a task
It explicitly gates downstream work on a decision URI
The resume action is delegated to the lane/operator rather than automated
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history
Body is Markdown prose explaining the signal meaning
Uses board:// and decision:// URI schemes
Follows a raised-state signal lifecycle pattern
notes: A noticeboard signal that the visual-fidelity decision is resolved and gated work may resume.
questions: What specific work items are currently gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
What concrete deliverables does 'Push the drawn style further first' require?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records and broadcasts the decision so gated work can resume.
intention_signals: To communicate that a pending global decision has been resolved and to unblock dependent lanes waiting for that decision.
contribution: Provides a durable, timestamped, URI-addressable signal that a global decision has been made and that gated work may resume, supporting coordination without embedding decision logic in code or tasks.
summary_for_embedding: A raised noticeboard signal recording the operator's decision for visual-fidelity to push the drawn style further first and indicating that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity can resume.
intention_for_embedding: To broadcast the resolution of the visual-fidelity decision and unblock dependent work.
